title: Tabs orientation prop is swallowed before reaching the Base UI primitive
reasoning: Tabs accepts the Base UI root props type, including orientation, but destructures orientation and only uses it for a data attribute. The remaining props spread no longer contains orientation, so Base UI cannot switch keyboard/navigation semantics for vertical tabs even though callers can pass orientation="vertical". This creates an API contract mismatch between the wrapper's type and the behavior of the wrapped primitive.
reproduction: Render <Tabs orientation="vertical"> with triggers. The DOM advertises data-orientation="vertical", but the underlying TabsPrimitive.Root is never passed orientation="vertical", so primitive behavior remains at its default.
recommendation: Pass orientation={orientation} to TabsPrimitive.Root in addition to any data attribute needed for styling.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for wrapper prop forwarding or vertical keyboard behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test rendering <Tabs orientation="vertical"> and assert the primitive receives vertical orientation semantics, ideally through expected keyboard navigation behavior rather than implementation details.
minimumFixScope: Forward the orientation prop from Tabs to TabsPrimitive.Root.
